artPrompt: An evocative scene inside a carnival mirror maze, a lone silhouette trapped at the center as dozens of warped reflections spiral away in vertiginous foreshortened corridors, each twin reaching back with grasping hands. Hot-gold and sickly-neon light fractures across the glass; smoky black seams divide the panes, scattered coins glint underfoot, claustrophobic and endless, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Mirror Maze of Debts
text:
Each opponent is lost in the mirror maze. At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, they roll a six-sided die to find the exit. On a 6 they escape and untap as normal. On anything else they're still in there: they skip their draw step and you draw a card, reflected back at you from a dozen wrong turns.
There is no exit on the half-step. There is half an exit, but it's a reflection.
flavorText: Every reflection owes you money. Even the ones that aren't yours. Especially those.

extras.dice: opponents roll to escape each upkeep
